2026-04-20T22:45:15.8295533Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-20T22:45:15.8296051Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-20T22:45:15.8296457Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-20T22:45:15.8296810Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-20T22:45:15.8297110Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-20T22:45:15.8297340Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-20T22:45:15.8297518Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-20T22:45:15.8297698Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-20T22:45:15.8297868Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-20T22:45:15.8298093Z [36;1m{[0m
2026-04-20T22:45:15.8298292Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_api_key=${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-20T22:45:15.8298570Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-20T22:45:15.8298820Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

Supports reply_to_id for discussion threading.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.","type":["number","string"]},"reply_to_id":{"description":"Node ID of the discussion comment to reply to, enabling threaded discussion comments. 2026-04-20T22:47:34.2509203Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-20T22:46:20.999187635Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58410,\"title\":\"[Core] Docs: Azure.Core 1.54.0-beta.1 CHANGELOG missing AppSettings schema bug fix entry\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.Core`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/core/Azure.Core/`\\n**Triggered by:** PR #58408 by `@m-nash`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #58408 fixed a bug in `sdk/core/Azure.Core/schema/ConfigurationSchema.json` where the top-level `definitions/credential` declared `required: [\\u0026#34;CredentialSource\\u0026#34;]` — the same property already required by `System.ClientModel`\\u0026#39;s schema segment. When the appsettings schema combiner merges segments by definition name, it concatenates `required` arrays without deduplication, producing `[\\u0026#34;CredentialSource\\u0026#34;, \\u0026#34;CredentialSource\\u0026#34;]` in the consumer\\u0026#39;s combined `AppSettingsSchema.json`. The fix removes the duplicate declaration from Azure.Core\\u0026#39;s schema segment.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- The `### Bugs Fixed` section under `## 1.54.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` in `sdk/core/Azure.Core/CHANGELOG.md` is empty — there is no entry documenting this fix.\\n- The `README.md` does not describe the AppSettings / `appsettings.json` IntelliSense schema feature at all (it was introduced in 1.52.0), so users encountering the duplicate-`required` issue have no documentation to reference.\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/core/Azure.Core/CHANGELOG.md`\\n**Action:** Edit — add a `Bugs Fixed` entry under `## 1.54.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)`.\\n\\nReplace the empty `### Bugs Fixed` section:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Bugs Fixed\\n```\\n\\nwith:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Bugs Fixed\\n\\n- Fixed `ConfigurationSchema.json` credential definition to remove a duplicate top-level `required: [\\u0026#34;CredentialSource\\u0026#34;]` entry. When multiple schema segments are merged into `AppSettingsSchema.json`, the combiner concatenates `required` arrays without deduplication, producing `[\\u0026#34;CredentialSource\\u0026#34;, \\u0026#34;CredentialSource\\u0026#34;]` in the combined file. Because `System.ClientModel` already asserts this requirement, removing it from Azure.Core\\u0026#39;s segment eliminates the duplicate with no change in validation behavior. ([#58408](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/pull/58408))\\n```\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md`\\n**Action:** Edit — add a section under `## Key concepts` (after the existing credential bullet) explaining the AppSettings JSON schema feature. Insert the following after the line about `DefaultAzureCredential`/`ManagedIdentityCredential` and before the `### Thread safety` heading:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Configuring clients via `appsettings.json` (IntelliSense schema)\\n\\nStarting with Azure.Core 1.52.0, the NuGet package ships a `ConfigurationSchema.json` segment. When a consumer references Azure.Core (directly or transitively), the build tooling merges these segments into `obj/{config}/{tfm}/AppSettingsSchema.json`. This enables:\\n\\n- **IntelliSense / autocompletion** for `AzureClients` configuration blocks in `appsettings.json` inside Visual Studio and VS Code.\\n- **Validation** of `Retry`, `Diagnostics`, and `CredentialSource` options at edit time.\\n\\nNo additional setup is required — the schema is automatically merged during `dotnet build`.\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nNo new executable snippets are required for this change. The CHANGELOG entry and README prose update above are sufficient.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README at `sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md` currently contains all required sections. No structural changes are needed beyond the new prose paragraph added in Step 1.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/core/Azure.Core/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/core/Azure.Core/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 core` — verify no unintended README changes.\\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 core` — no public API changes expected; verify the output is unchanged.\\n5. `dotnet format sdk/core/Azure.Core/`\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!TIP]\\n\\u0026gt; **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!WARNING]\\n\\u0026gt; \\n\\u0026gt; \\u003cstrong\\u003e⚠️ Firewall blocked 2 domains\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; The following domains were blocked by the firewall during workflow execution:\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; - `pkgs.dev.azure.com`\\n\\u0026gt; - `releaseassets.githubusercontent.com`\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; To allow these domains, add them to the `network.allowed` list in your workflow frontmatter:\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; ```yaml\\n\\u0026gt; network:\\n\\u0026gt;   allowed:\\n\\u0026gt;     - defaults\\n\\u0026gt;     - \\u0026#34;pkgs.dev.azure.com\\u0026#34;\\n\\u0026gt;     - \\u0026#34;releaseassets.githubusercontent.com\\u0026#34;\\n\\u0026gt; ```\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; See [Network Configuration](https://github.github.com/gh-aw/reference/network/) for more information.\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; \\n\\n\\n\\u0026gt; Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24693941236/agentic_workflow) · ● 408.9K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22\\u0026amp;type=issues)\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58410\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"github-actions[bot]\",\"id\":41898282,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/apps/github-actions\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"NONE\",\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":0,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":0},\"created_at\":\"2026-04-20T22:43:20Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-20T22:43:20Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}

The fix removes the duplicate declaration from Azure.Core\\u0026#39;s schema segment.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- The `### Bugs Fixed` section under `## 1.54.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` in `sdk/core/Azure.Core/CHANGELOG.md` is empty — there is no entry documenting this fix.\\n- The `README.md` does not describe the AppSettings / `appsettings.json` IntelliSense schema feature at all (it was introduced in 1.52.0), so users encountering the duplicate-`required` issue have no documentation to reference.\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/core/Azure.Core/CHANGELOG.md`\\n**Action:** Edit — add a `Bugs Fixed` entry under `## 1.54.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)`.\\n\\nReplace the empty `### Bugs Fixed` section:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Bugs Fixed\\n```\\n\\nwith:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Bugs Fixed\\n\\n- Fixed `ConfigurationSchema.json` credential definition to remove a duplicate top-level `required: [\\u0026#34;CredentialSource\\u0026#34;]` entry.
